dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses N-bar lookback for new highs/lows, which maps to Donchian channel (dc_upper/dc_lower). Default N=10 bars. Entry on new high or new low interpreted as context-dependent long/short. Exit conditions were AUTHOR_SILENT, so reversal logic applied: exit long on new low, exit short on new high. Intraday scope is implicit in backtest engine configuration.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses N-bar lookback for new highs/lows, which maps to Donchian channel (dc_upper/dc_lower). Default N=10 bars. Entry on new high or new low interpreted as context-dependent long/short. Exit conditions were AUTHOR_SILENT, so reversal logic applied: exit long on new low, exit short on new high. Intraday scope is implicit in backtest engine configuration.

source_rule_text: Enter on new highs or new lows of the previous N bars during the intraday session; exit rules are not explicitly stated.
